examples.title: Basic String Functions - Length, Copy, and Concatenation
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental string manipulation functions: strlen(), strcpy(), and strcat()
examples.explanation:
1. strlen() calculates the number of characters in str1 excluding null terminator
2. strcpy() copies contents of str1 into str3
3. strcat() appends str2 to str3 after adding a space
4. Each function requires <string.h> header file

examples.title: String Comparison and Substring Search
examples.description: Demonstrates strcmp() for string comparison and custom substring search function
examples.explanation:
1. strcmp() compares strings lexicographically, returns 0 if equal
2. strstr() returns pointer to first occurrence of substring or NULL
3. Custom function uses strstr() to check substring existence
4. Difference in pointers gives the position of substring
examples.timeComplexity: O(n*m) for comparison, O(n) for substring search

examples.title: String Reversal and Palindrome Check
examples.description: Advanced string manipulation: reversing a string and checking if it's a palindrome
examples.explanation:
1. reverseString() uses two-pointer technique to swap characters from both ends
2. isPalindrome() compares characters from both ends, converting to lowercase for case-insensitive check
3. strtok() breaks string into tokens using delimiter
4. All operations work in-place without extra memory
